page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base frequency of 3.1 GHz. The system is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core tests, falls within the average range. However, multi-core benchmarks, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, are positioned in the bottom 10% due to the low overall core count. Memory bandwidth tests also register in the lower percentiles, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 13.43 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.hfr6.large offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute capacity without the overhead of high core counts. This makes it suitable for lightweight memory-bound applications, small-scale caching layers, or development environments running database engines. However, it is not suited for compute-intensive multi-threaded tasks, high-throughput web serving, or large-scale data processing due to its constrained multi-core performance and baseline network limits.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY x86_64 processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per core, while lacking local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers average single-core CPU performance but exhibits lower multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities in benchmark testing. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive workloads that do not demand extensive parallel processing. It is best suited for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
